channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Florida GOP pushes for government efficiency with DOGE-inspired legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, also known as DOGE, has stirred up controversy as it searches for wasteful federal spending. Now, Florida GOP lawmakers are planning a similar initiative.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Justice Department official urges quick dismissal of criminal case against New York City mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In an extraordinary courtroom showdown, a federal judge pressed the Justice Department on Wednesday on its demand to throw out New York City Mayor Eric Adams’ corruption case, grilling a top official and putting the Democratic mayor under oath to answer concerns that the Trump admini
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth directs Pentagon to find $50 billion in cuts this year to fund Trump military priorities
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has directed the military services to identify $50 billion in programs that could be cut next year in order to redirect those savings to fund President Donald Trump's priorities.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fetterman loses two top staffers as he makes waves by bucking Democratic Party
item.description: Renegade Pennsylvania Democrat John Fetterman is suffering more staff departures as they continue to color his time in the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin lays out roles of 'probationary employees' as Dems claim critical employees are being cut
item.description: The Trump administration explained to Fox News Digital that probationary employees of the federal government, who are facing mass terminations, are not ensured permanent employment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Agriculture Department tries to rehire fired workers tied to bird flu response
item.description: The Agriculture Department is scrambling to rehire several workers it fired as part of President Donald Trump’s broad cuts because they were involved in the government’s response to the ongoing bird flu outbreak that has devastated egg and poultry farms over the past three years.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gov. Gavin Newsom proposes $125M mortgage relief program to help victims of recent natural disasters
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom on Wednesday proposed a new $125 million package to help residents whose homes were destroyed or damaged by recent natural disasters, including the recent wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin directs federal agencies to end DEI considerations in govt contracts as DOGE's impact spreads
item.description: The Trump administration told federal contractors this week to stop considering DEI when handing out government contracts, in an effort to follow through on Trump's Day One executive order
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cuts to Veterans Affairs made in 'dishonest' way: Former employee
item.description: Employees fired included researchers looking into cancer treatments, opioid addiction and burn pit exposure.
item.dc:creator: Nichole Berlie, NewsNation

item.title: No decision from judge on DOJ request to drop case against Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The federal judge considering the Justice Department's request to dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams said Wednesday that he won't rule immediately and will take time to review all materials. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Some of the 400 jobs that were cut at the FAA helped support air safety, a union says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration has said no one at the Federal Aviation Administration with a “critical safety” position has been fired as it cuts the federal workforce, but some FAA jobs that were eliminated had direct roles in supporting safety inspectors and airport oper
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Kelly Loeffler to lead Small Business Administration
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed former Sen. Kelly Loeffler (R-Ga.) to lead the Small Business Administration (SBA), keeping up a frantic pace to cement President Trump's team in place. Senators voted 52-46 on Loeffler's nomination.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal worker says he was fired despite taking Trump's buyout offer
item.description: A former federal employee says he was fired after accepting a buyout offer from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Trump warns Zelenskyy to quickly negotiate war's end with Russia or risk not having a nation to lead
item.description: President Donald Trump has warned Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy that he “better move fast” to negotiate an end to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine or risk not having a nation to lead.
item.dc:creator: Stephany Matat And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge to weigh arguments for dropping NYC Mayor Eric Adams' charges
item.description: Adams pleaded not guilty in September to allegations that he accepted at least $100K in illegal campaign contributions.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: Trump wants to lower the cost of IVF. A new executive order seeks ideas to do that
item.description: President Trump campaigned on lowering the cost of IVF, after a controversial ruling made the treatment stop in Alabama for a short time.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin and Diane Webber, MPR News

item.title: Trails, bathrooms, wait times: How National Park staffing cuts could impact your next visit
item.description: While thousands of seasonal staff are expected to be hired, the loss of other employees could make your next national park visit feel different.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Howard Lutnick as Commerce secretary
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed Howard Lutnick to serve as Commerce secretary, approving a key member of President Trump’s economic team amid rising trade tensions. Senators voted 51-45 in favor of Lutnick’s confirmation to lead the Commerce Department, which has broad authority over U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, NewsNation

item.title: Judge refuses to block Musk, DOGE from 7 federal agencies
item.description: U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan on Tuesday refused the request of 14 Democratic state attorneys general to immediately impose wide-ranging restrictions on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: How President Trump is reshaping transgender people’s rights
item.description: MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests break down President Donald Trump’s series of executive orders that significantly reshape transgender rights in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Nikhil Kumaran, MPR News

item.title: U.S. and Russia discuss ending Ukraine war, without Kyiv
item.description: The talks build on last week’s phone call between President Trump and Russian leader Vladimir Putin during which the two leaders agreed to work together toward a negotiated end to the war.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes, MPR News

item.title: Mexico’s President Sheinbaum threatens to take legal action over the Gulf name change
item.description: Earlier this month, Sheinbaum shared a letter addressed to Google with reporters, arguing that the U.S. had no authority to unilaterally rename the Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Manuela López Restrepo, MPR News

item.title: As sports betting has soared, more people search online for help with gambling addiction
item.description: A study shows more people are looking for help to manage gambling addiction, in the years after a Supreme Court decision allowed online sports betting in 38 states.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle, MPR News

item.title: New Florida Attorney General sworn in, first priority illegal immigration
item.description: Florida’s new Attorney General has been sworn in and he's hitting the ground, running. After taking the oath of office, James Uthmeier stood with Gov. Ron DeSantis and the cabinet holding an emergency meeting to implement immigration enforcement measures.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Muller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Steven Joachim, Fox 5

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: NAACP spending guide urges Black Americans to avoid companies that dumped DEI
item.description: The nation’s oldest civil rights organization released its Black Consumer Advisory, a project intended to help educate Black Americans on “who’s pushing progress and who’s stuck in the past.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: How a South Texas community college embraced apprenticeships to ease a growing nursing shortage
item.description: South Texas College in McAllen launched one of the first registered nursing apprenticeships in the country as area hospitals expect the need for nurses to increase.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Sneha Dey, KPRC 2

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Legislature cancels meetings, closes building as Sierra storm arrives in Nevada
item.description: While Las Vegas drivers navigate rain-soaked roads for the first time in awhile, the Nevada Legislature has shut down for the day to keep drivers out of hazardous conditions in the Reno-Carson City area.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Could Kamala Harris be the next California governor? Here's how she's polling
item.description: Former Vice President and Democratic Presidential candidate Kamala Harris holds a substantial lead over other potential candidates to succeed California Gov. Gavin Newsom in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5
